label_definition: 1 = persona-implied risk band is WATCH or HIGH_RISK (declining_stress, concentration_risk, inflated_gst_fraud); 0 = EXCELLENT or GOOD (healthy_growth, seasonal_stable).
temporal_split: features computed from months 0-17 only (sub-scores recomputed on a truncated FeatureBundle); the label is persona-implied, not derived from the held-out months — no direct leakage from the tail of each applicant's history.
excluded_reason: ntc_thin_file persona has only 8 months of history — too short for the 18/6 split.
honest_note: Trained and evaluated ENTIRELY on the 60 synthetic seeded personas (RANDOM_SEED=42). Demonstrates that the six engineered sub-scores carry separable signal for persona-implied risk buckets on this seeded population (test_accuracy=0.867, test_auc=1.0). This is a feature sanity-check on 50 samples, NOT a claim of real-world credit accuracy — the UI/README must present it as 'model agreement', never as an accuracy percentage. IMPORTANT: this classifier is not the fraud-detection mechanism. Even where fraud_persona_recall_in_test=1.0 is high on this run's split, that coverage is incidental (sub-scores can pick up secondary symptoms of the fraud persona's stress) and unverified at scale — it is NOT guaranteed the way validation/cross_checks.py is, which deterministically flags 100% of the fraud persona via the GST-declared-vs-observed-inflows check (see test_cross_checks.py). Fraud review decisions must always route through cross_checks.py, never through this model.
